name: Environment Inheritance Leak
summary: Agentic frameworks pass the full parent process environment (os.environ.copy()) to spawned subprocesses — including MCP servers, code interpreters, and tool executors — exposing all API keys, database credentials, and cloud access tokens to untrusted or third-party code.
mechanism: When spawning MCP server subprocesses via stdio (e.g., MCP('npx -y @smithery/cli ...')), the framework copies all parent environment variables. Third-party or compromised packages executed via npx/pipx can read os.environ and silently exfiltrate OPENAI_API_KEY, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, DATABASE_URL, and all other secrets.
blast_radius: All environment variables accessible to the parent process are exposed to untrusted subprocesses. Supply chain attacks can silently exfiltrate credentials via HTTP callbacks.
prerequisite: Framework that spawns subprocesses for tool/MCP execution. Secrets stored in environment variables (standard practice).

evidence.key_value: 100% of parent environment variables inherited by subprocess

defences.name: Environment Allowlisting
defences.description: Pass only explicitly whitelisted environment variables (PATH, HOME, LANG) to subprocesses. Require explicit opt-in for each additional variable.

defences.name: Sensitive Pattern Filtering
defences.description: Strip variables matching *_KEY, *_SECRET, *_TOKEN, *_PASSWORD, *_CREDENTIAL from subprocess environments
defences.effectiveness: Reduces credential exposure with heuristic matching

cwe_mapping: CWE-200 (Exposure of Sensitive Information), CWE-214 (Invocation of Process Using Visible Sensitive Information)

poc.author: l3tchupkt (original reporter)
poc.description: MCP('python -c "import os,json; print(json.dumps(dict(os.environ)))"') dumps all parent env vars including OPENAI_API_KEY
